channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump administration rolls back a key protection for imperiled wildlife
item.description: The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act. The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act. The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law.

(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Poor counties adjust to cuts in food stamp program
item.description: Poor counties in the South are bracing for steep cuts in administering food aid as President Trump's tax and spending bill brings new requirements.
item.content:encoded: Poor counties in the South are bracing for steep cuts in administering food aid as President Trump's tax and spending bill brings new requirements.
item.dc:creator: Nick de la Canal

item.title: Graham Platner makes it official in Maine, submitting paperwork to leave Senate race
item.description: In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.
item.content:encoded:
In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.

(Image credit: CJ Gunther)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Utah judge now considering evidence against accused killer of Charlie Kirk
item.description: A weeklong hearing to establish whether the accused killer of conservative activist Charlie Kirk can be taken to full trial ended today. There has been dramatic testimony.
item.content:encoded: A weeklong hearing to establish whether the accused killer of conservative activist Charlie Kirk can be taken to full trial ended today. There has been dramatic testimony.
item.dc:creator: Sean Higgins

item.title: Immigrants who were detained during fatal shooting in Houston dispute ICE's account
item.description: Three men who were arrested during an immigration operation which resulted in the death of another person are disputing ICE's version of responding to a situation in which the agents felt at risk,
item.content:encoded: Three men who were arrested during an immigration operation which resulted in the death of another person are disputing ICE's version of responding to a situation in which the agents felt at risk,
item.dc:creator: Bianca Seward

item.title: Patriotic art gets the spotlight as NEA funding shifts. Cue 'The Ronald Reagan Overture'
item.description: Patriotic art and music is taking center stage this year under the Trump Administration, as funds shift away from DEI. For some orgs, like the Reagan Presidential Library, this is their wheelhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Patriotic art and music is taking center stage this year under the Trump Administration, as funds shift away from DEI. For some orgs, like the Reagan Presidential Library, this is their wheelhouse.

(Image credit: Consolidated News Pictures/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Houston neighbors started seeing more ICE agents around. Then came a fatal shooting.
item.description: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was a 52-year-old Mexican national who worked in construction for more than three decades. The father of three was shot by Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents after they attempted to pull him over.
item.content:encoded:
Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was a 52-year-old Mexican national who worked in construction for more than three decades. The father of three was shot by Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents after they attempted to pull him over.

(Image credit: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán
)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Waymo called the cops on teen riders, raising privacy concerns
item.description: Two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from a driverless taxi when the company disabled it and alerted police.
item.content:encoded:
Two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from a driverless taxi when the company disabled it and alerted police.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: No internet, no screen time? FCC weighs cutting subsidy that lowers school internet bills
item.description: Many schools rely on consumer fees funneled through the federal government to cut internet costs. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr called for ending this program before Donald Trump tapped him for the job.
item.content:encoded:
Many schools rely on consumer fees funneled through the federal government to cut internet costs. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr called for ending this program before Donald Trump tapped him for the job.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: A federal panel advances Trump's proposed arch, despite unanswered questions
item.description: The National Capital Planning Commission greenlit preliminary site and building plans after hours of public pushback. It wants more details before what could be its final review in September.
item.content:encoded: The National Capital Planning Commission greenlit preliminary site and building plans after hours of public pushback. It wants more details before what could be its final review in September.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
